mnemonics.title: RGB Color Formation
mnemonics.content: Red + Green + Blue = White (additive light). Remember: Think of a TV screen in a dark room - all colors combined give white.

mnemonics.title: Sampling vs Quantization
mnemonics.content: SAMPLING: Space (where pixels go). QUANTIZATION: Shades (gray levels). Think: 'SamPle has Position, Quantize has Quality (gray levels).'

mnemonics.title: Pixel Depth Memory
mnemonics.content: 1-bit = 2 colors (Binary), 8-bit = 256 levels (Grayscale), 24-bit = 16.7M colors (True Color). Powers of 2: 2ⁿ determines color levels.

mnemonics.title: Connectivity Types
mnemonics.content: 4-connect: North, South, East, West (cross shape). 8-connect: All 8 directions (box around pixel).

acronyms.usage: Additive color model for displays, where colors are created by combining light intensities.
Subtractive color model for printing, where inks absorb light to produce colors.
Windows bitmap image format, typically uncompressed.
Lossy compression format for photographs, uses DCT.
High-quality lossless format for professional imaging.
Color model representing colors in terms of human perception.

visualTips.visualization: Think of image as a grid/matrix. Pixel (i,j) means row i, column j. Like Excel sheet cells.
Left peak = dark image, Right peak = bright image, Middle peak = dull/gray image, Spread = high contrast.
Stack 8 transparent sheets. Top (MSB) shows main structure, bottom (LSB) shows noise patterns. Higher planes = more visual info.
Imagine zooming in: More samples = smoother curves, Fewer samples = blocky/jagged appearance.

analogies.analogy: Like pixels are to image as atoms are to matter - fundamental building blocks.
Drawing on grid paper vs blank paper - grid determines precision of your drawing.
Limited colors (quantization) = box of 12 pencils vs 48 pencils - less shades possible.
High resolution = sharp detailed photo, Low resolution = blurry or pixelated image.
Like human ear can hear quiet to loud sounds, dynamic range measures darkest to brightest perceivable.
